step_plan.summary: When update_fields is passed to post_save_handler, restrict the previous serialized state to only the fields present in the new serialized state before calling diff so that non-updated fields are not reported as changed.

steps.operation: Filter previous serialized state when update_fields is provided before computing diff
steps.reason: post_save_handler previously computed prev_state from instance._previous_state (full state) and new_state via serialize_simple(instance, update_fields) (partial state). The diff(before, after) iterates over all keys in before, so keys missing from after were treated as changed (new_value=None). By restricting prev_state to only the keys present in new_state (or the requested update_fields) before calling diff, we ensure only actually-updated fields are compared and returned in the diff.
steps.side_effects: Behavior: The diff returned in thread_kwargs will now only include fields that were serialized into new_state (i.e., updated fields). Tests: fixes test_update and preserves test_serialize_fields. Dependents: code that consumes the generated diff will now no longer see spurious 'field -> null' changes for non-updated fields; any consumer relying on previously buggy diffs will see different (correct) results. API: no external API changes, only correctness of audit diffs. Edge cases: update_fields entries that are not serialized (deferred fields or expressions) will simply be absent from new_state and thus not present in the filtered prev_state — no exception is raised. Created=True behavior is unchanged (prev_state remains empty). M2M handling is unaffected because m2m diffs are handled in a separate handler.
